description_long: This house features a modern, boxy architectural design with a combination of exposed brick and wood paneling on its exterior. The structure is composed of several rectangular volumes, some of which are taller and more prominent, creating a staggered, multi-block appearance. Large windows are set into the wood-paneled sections, providing ample natural light and a contemporary aesthetic. Mature trees are integrated into the layout, both in front and between the building volumes, enhancing the connection to nature and offering privacy. The flat roofs and open terraces suggest potential for rooftop use or gardens. The overall look is minimalist, with clean lines and a focus on geometric forms.
description: A modern, multi-volume house with exposed brick, wood paneling, large windows, and flat roofs, surrounded by mature trees. The design is minimalist and geometric, with integrated greenery.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, wood-paneled facade, large windows, and several mature trees in front.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tall brick volumes and dense tree coverage between the building sections.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the brick exterior, a wood-paneled section with windows, and trees partially obscuring the structure.
description_view_3: Angled view showing the interplay of brick and wood, flat roofs, and the arrangement of trees around the house.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with large picture windows
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway features a large glass door set in a wood-paneled section, with no visible steps or porch, and is flanked by mature trees.
